evidence.scientific_clarity: 30-node network with 4 communities rendered via Fruchterman-Reingold spring layout, colored by community — fully satisfies the brief's requirements.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set; PDF uses /Subtype /Type3 (bitmapped glyphs) and SVG uses DejaVuSans glyph paths — text is not selectable/editable; default DejaVu Sans font used throughout.
evidence.palette: 4-color categorical palette (#E63946 red, #457B9D blue, #2A9D8F teal, #E9C46A yellow) — no directional misuse; red-teal pair may be problematic for deuteranopes but blue/yellow anchor is safe.
evidence.layout_density: 7×7 inch canvas with tight_layout; edge alpha=0.25 reduces hairball; node labels at font_size=6 are small but legible; no obvious overlapping or gaping whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and legend is placed lower-right; however, caption contains no numerical finding (no edge count, modularity score, or quantitative claim) that Reviewer 2 would expect.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is version 1.4 with real vector paths for edges/nodes, but fonts are Type3 bitmapped glyphs (not TrueType/Type1); script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (119 KB, 1035×1035 px), caption.tex (350 B), gen_figure.py (1.7 KB); bonus figure.svg also produced.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption describes the algorithm (Fruchterman-Reingold, seed 42) and visual choices (edge opacity) but provides no specific numerical finding or scientific claim about the community structure.

overall_strengths: Fully satisfies the brief: 30-node network, 4 communities, force-directed layout, hairball mitigation via edge alpha and node sizing.
All required output files are present and non-trivial; clean categorical palette with no directional color misuse.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 never set — PDF and SVG embed DejaVu Sans as Type3 bitmapped glyph outlines, making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector editors.
Caption lacks any quantitative finding (e.g., number of edges, inter-community edge fraction, or modularity), which Reviewer 2 would flag as insufficient for a scientific figure.
